title: helenium auriculatum
intro: Helenium auriculatum, also known as American or dropcone sneezeweed, is a perennial herb native to eastern North America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Helenium auriculatum can be prepared into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes, although its effectiveness and safety as a treatment for specific health conditions should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.
